question: Explain why the solution formed when magnesium nitride reacts with water is alkaline.
full_answer: Magnesium nitride reacts with water to produce ammonia. Ammonia reacts with water to form hydroxide ions. Therefore the solution is alkaline.

rounds.prompt: Magnesium nitride reacts with water to produce _____. Ammonia reacts with water to form hydroxide ions. Therefore the solution is alkaline.

question: Explain why bromine water is decolourised by an alkene.
full_answer: The alkene reacts with bromine in an addition reaction. Bromine is used up as the carbon-carbon double bond is broken and new carbon-bromine bonds are formed. Therefore the orange colour disappears.

question: Explain why a carbonate effervesces when dilute acid is added.
full_answer: Carbonate ions react with hydrogen ions to form carbon dioxide gas and water. The carbon dioxide escapes as bubbles. Therefore effervescence is seen.

question: Explain why acidified potassium dichromate(VI) changes from orange to green when it reacts with an aldehyde.
full_answer: The aldehyde is oxidised to a carboxylic acid. At the same time dichromate(VI) ions are reduced to chromium(III) ions, which are green in solution. Therefore the solution changes from orange to green.
